---
title: "Clara RANKL RNAseq Analysis"
output:
  html_notebook: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

**Updated by AJB Dec 18 2024**

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook of
Andrew's work on ____________


An diagram of the overall RNAseq workflow is shown below. Steps in
yellow were completed prior to this notebook. Steps in light blue are
completed below. Teal squares represent intermediate files generated
throughout the pipeline.

![]
(RNASeq_Pipeline.png)

**Required Files For this Notebook**

-   The mapped reads, stored as *.bam* files.
-   The reference genome, converted to *.gtf* format.

### RNAseq Counts and Differential Expression

Begin by installing and loading packages. The RNAseq analysis uses the
package
[Rsubread](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/Rsubread/inst/doc/SubreadUsersGuide.pdf)
to generate mRNA counts for each gene. Then, this pipeline uses
[DESeq2](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/manuals/DESeq2/man/DESeq2.pdf)
in order to determine differential expression of genes.

```{r}
# IF INSTALLATION IS NECESSARY
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#     install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("Rsubread")
# BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
# BiocManager::install("apeglm")
# BiocManager::install("biomaRt")

# Load Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(Rsubread)
library(DESeq2)
library(apeglm)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(biomaRt)
```

Access the aligned reads (SAM format) and run `Rsubread-featureCounts`
to determine how many RNA reads align to each gene.

```{r}
# Generate list of SAM files for featureCounts to use.
align_directory<-"RNAseq_Preprocess/data/aligned"
files<-list.files(path=align_directory, full.names=TRUE, pattern="\\.bam$")

# Run featureCounts on each file.
counts <- featureCounts(files=files,
                        isGTFAnnotationFile=TRUE,
                        annot.ext="Mus_musculus.GRCm39.113.gtf",
                        GTF.featureType="exon",
                        GTF.attrType="gene_id",
                        isPairedEnd=TRUE,
                        genome="Mus_musculus.GRCm39.dna_rm.primary_assembly.fa",
                        minFragLength=50,
                        nthreads=8,
                        )

```

Now save the featureCounts object to file. This .txt file will be a
matrix with each gene, and the number of assigned counts from each input file.

This is a savepoint - and I've transferred this data to the INF drive. I
will not upload the .fastq or .sam files to the INF drive to save \>
20GB of space. As mentioned above the raw data is all available in the
SRA, and my preprocessing pipeline is reproducible in Snakemake.

```{r}
# Write the featureCounts object to a .txt file.
# The lapply function removes weird footers from gene names.
write.table(
  x=data.frame(counts$annotation["GeneID"],
    counts$counts,
    stringsAsFactors=FALSE) %>%
    lapply(gsub, pattern='.t01', replacement='') %>%
    lapply(gsub, pattern='.r01', replacement=''),
  file="counts.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t",
  row.names=FALSE)
```

Read in the counts matrix and load into DESeq2.

```{r}
# Import counts file as well as column data (which links SRA acquisition number with experimental condition)
cts <- as.matrix(read.table("counts.txt",sep="\t", header=TRUE))
coldata <- as.matrix(read.table("seq_reference.txt", sep="\t", header=TRUE))

# Extract counts & Ignore first column (geneID) and first row (sample names)
countDataMatrix <- as.matrix(cts[-1,-1])
# Convert to integer
mode(countDataMatrix) <- "integer"
# Set rownames to geneIDs
rownames(countDataMatrix) <- cts[-1,1]
# Set colnames to sample names
colnames(countDataMatrix) <- colnames(cts)[-1]   

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=countDataMatrix, 
                              colData=coldata,
                              design = ~ condition)
```

Now a brief filtering step to remove rows that have fewer than 10 reads.

```{r}
# Filter rows with less than 10 reads.
smallestGroupSize <- 3
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= smallestGroupSize
dds <- dds[keep,]
```

Now we run DESeq to determine differentially-expressed genes.

```{r}
# Run DESeq2 for each of the comparisons:
dds$condition = relevel(dds$condition, ref = "4h_mock_0")
dds_4h_M_0 <- DESeq(dds)

```
```{r}
resultsNames(dds_4h_M_0)
```
Now we use a log fold change (LFC) shrinkage correction to improve gene
ranks. This was published in [*Bioinformatics* in 2018.](https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895)

```{r}
# Use the LFC adjustment for each group.
Stm_4h_0LFC <- lfcShrink(dds_4h_M_0,coef="condition_4h_STm_0_vs_4h_mock_0", type="apeglm")
```

Now we can preview the summarized data, one by one. These data are
listed in gene order to begin with. These commands also save the
resulting data as *.csv* files for future reference.

This first block also imports a reference *.csv* generated from the `gene_list.ipynb` Python notebook. This notebook reads the *.gb* file and relates the locus id (i.e. "BSR30_RS00010") to gene name and gene product. We will merge this reference with the output folder to facilitate plotting.

**Rel-Syn**

```{r}
grcm39 = useEnsembl(biomart='ensembl', dataset="mmusculus_gene_ensembl")

attributeList <- listAttributes(grcm39)
```


```{r}
all_genes <- getBM(attributes=c('ensembl_gene_id','gene_biotype','external_gene_name','chromosome_name','start_position','end_position'), mart = grcm39)

write.table(all_genes, file="grcm39_ref.txt",
  quote=FALSE,
  sep="\t",
  row.names=FALSE)
```



```{r}
# Import reference .csv.
ref <- read.csv("Sau_HG001_reference.csv")
ref <- as.matrix(ref)
# Set locus tags (first column) as row names.
refMatrix <- ref[,-1]
rownames(refMatrix) <- ref[,1]

# Create matrix from Rel-Syn results.
Syn <- as.matrix(relSynLFC)
# Merge matrix with reference.
SynRef <- merge(refMatrix,Syn,by="row.names",all.x=TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  drop_na()

# Rename "row.names" column to Locus
colnames(SynRef)[colnames(SynRef)=="Row.names"] <- "locus"

# Save .csv for Rel-Syn results.
write.csv(SynRef, file="RelSyn_results.csv", row.names=FALSE)
SynRef
```

### Plotting

Start with a volcano plot to visualize up- and downregulation.
```{r}

SynRefSignif <- transform(SynRef, signif= ifelse(log2FoldChange>=log2(3) | log2FoldChange<=-log2(3) & padj<.001, TRUE, FALSE))

up = length(which(SynRefSignif$signif == TRUE & SynRefSignif$log2FoldChange>=log2(3)))
down = length(which(SynRefSignif$signif == TRUE & SynRefSignif$log2FoldChange<=-log2(3)))

plot <- ggplot(data=SynRefSignif, aes(x=log2FoldChange, y=-log10(padj), color=signif)) +
  geom_point()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE"="grey", "TRUE"="black")) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title="Differentially Expressed Genes after Rel-Syn Activation",
       subtitle= paste(up,"Genes Upregulated,",down,"Genes Downregulated"),
       color="Significance")

plot

```
